docs.title: Speclite
docs.text:
AI-native local-first specification state management CLI.

Speclite treats specifications as state, not documents. Inspired by Terraform's plan/apply workflow, it provides a safe, reviewable pipeline for evolving structured specifications from unstructured prose.

docs.title: Markdown is not canonical
docs.text:
Markdown is an editing projection — a human-friendly view of state. The canonical source of truth is the SQLite database.

Markdown / Plain Text
  ↓  import
Normalized Spec State
  ↓  plan
Diff / Pending Changes
  ↓  apply
SQLite State
  ↓  render
Markdown / Plain Text

Markdown is always regeneratable from state. Never edit rendered Markdown as your primary workflow; import it back through the normalizer instead.

docs.title: Specification is stateful
docs.text:
A specification is not a document — it is a node in a state machine with a defined lifecycle:

draft → review → fixed → implemented → verified → deprecated

Every transition is recorded in the immutable event log.

docs.title: AI-first workflow
docs.text: Speclite does not require strictly structured input. You can write rough plain text or Markdown, and the normalizer will infer IDs, titles, kinds, and relations. This makes it suitable for AI-generated content that needs to be integrated into a managed specification baseline.

docs.title: Preserve formalizability
docs.text: The data model is designed so that specifications can eventually be exported to formal languages (Alloy, TLA+, SMT-LIB, Lean, Datalog) for model checking and theorem proving. This is a non-goal for the MVP but is a first-class architectural concern.

docs.title: Design Principles (Terraform Analogy)
docs.text: Terraform concept Speclite equivalent Desired infrastructure Markdown / plain-text spec files State file state.sqlite Plan state.plan.json Apply State transition via speqlite apply Output speqlite render

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# Initialise a new Speclite workspace
speqlite init

# Import rough Markdown into a pending plan
speqlite import scratch/my-feature.md

# Review the pending changes
speqlite plan

# Commit the plan to state
speqlite apply

# Regenerate Markdown projections from state
speqlite render --all

# Full-text search
speqlite search "plan apply"

# Inspect dependency graph
speqlite deps CMD-APPLY

# Validate structural integrity
speqlite validate

docs.title: Primary Goals
docs.text:
• Local-first — everything runs offline, zero cloud dependency
  • CLI-only — composable with shell scripts and CI pipelines
  • Git-friendly — state snapshot and plan are plain JSON, diffable
  • AI-readable — structured JSON and Markdown outputs feed LLM context windows
  • Lightweight — single binary, embedded SQLite, no daemon
  • Structured specification state — typed nodes, typed relations, constraints
  • Full-text search — SQLite FTS5 with BM25 ranking
  • Dependency tracking — depends_on, implements, verifies, supersedes, related_to
  • Plan/apply workflow — no mutation without review
  • Markdown projection — human-editable, regeneratable

docs.title: Non-Goals (MVP)
docs.text:
• Web UI
  • SaaS / multi-user collaboration
  • RBAC
  • Real-time sync
  • Graph / vector databases
  • Embedding / semantic search
  • Automatic proof generation
  • Workflow engine
  • External integrations (CI webhooks, etc.)

docs.title: Navigation
docs.text: Section Description Data Model SQLite schema: tables, columns, constraints, FTS State Machine Spec lifecycle: states, transitions, rules CLI Reference Every command, flag, exit code, and example Normalizer How prose is parsed into structured spec nodes Plan & Apply Plan diff format and apply workflow File Structure .spec/, specs/, scratch/ layout Future Extensions MCP server and formal language export

docs.title: CLI Reference
docs.text: Complete reference for all speqlite commands, flags, exit codes, and output formats.

docs.title: Global Flags
docs.text:
These flags are available on every command.

Flag Type Default Description --workspace string . Path to the workspace root (must contain .spec/) --format string text Output format: text, json, json-pretty --quiet bool false Suppress informational output; only errors go to stderr --no-color bool false Disable ANSI colour codes --verbose bool false Increase log verbosity --version — — Print version and exit --help, -h — — Print command help and exit

docs.title: Exit Codes
docs.text: Code Meaning 0 Success 1 General error (I/O, parsing, unexpected) 2 Validation error (structural or constraint violation) 3 No pending plan (used by apply when there is nothing to apply) 4 Conflict detected (apply would overwrite a concurrent change) 5 Workspace not initialised (.spec/ directory missing)

docs.title: speqlite init
docs.text: Initialise a new Speclite workspace in the current directory.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: speqlite init [flags]

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Type Default Description --force bool false Re-initialise an existing workspace (non-destructive)

docs.title: Behaviour
docs.text:
1. Creates .spec/ directory.
  2. Creates .spec/state.sqlite with the full DDL schema.
  3. Writes PRAGMA user_version = 1 to state.sqlite.
  4. Creates empty specs/, scratch/, and changes/ directories.
  5. Writes a change_status-equivalent init_workspace event to event_log.
  6. Prints confirmation to stdout.

docs.title: Output (text)
docs.text:
Initialised Speclite workspace at /path/to/project
  .spec/state.sqlite   created
  specs/               created
  scratch/             created
  changes/             created

docs.title: Exit Codes
docs.text: Code Condition 0 Success 1 Directory not writable 5 Already initialised and --force not set

docs.title: speqlite import
docs.text: Parse one or more input files and compute a pending plan. Does not mutate state.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: speqlite import <file> [<file>...] [flags]

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Type Default Description --dry-run bool false Compute plan but do not write state.plan.json --id-prefix string "" Override the ID prefix used when generating new IDs --kind string "" Force all parsed nodes to this kind (overrides inference) --status string draft Initial status for newly created specs

docs.title: Behaviour
docs.text:
1. Reads each file and dispatches to the appropriate parser (Markdown or plain text).
  2. Runs the normalizer to produce a list of candidate SpecNode values.
  3. Compares candidate nodes against the current state snapshot.
  4. Produces a plan (list of create, update, delete operations).
  5. Writes the plan to .spec/state.plan.json (unless --dry-run).
  6. Prints a summary of the plan.

docs.title: Output (text)
docs.text:
Parsed scratch/my-feature.md → 3 spec nodes

Plan:
  + create CMD-IMPORT        [command]  "Import command"
  + create CMD-APPLY         [command]  "Apply command"
  ~ update FR-001            [requirement]  "Functional Requirement 1"

3 changes pending. Run `speqlite apply` to commit.

docs.title: Output (json)
docs.text:
{
  "source_files": ["scratch/my-feature.md"],
  "plan_hash": "sha256:abc123...",
  "ops": [
    {"op": "create", "id": "CMD-IMPORT", "kind": "command", "title": "Import command"},
    {"op": "create", "id": "CMD-APPLY",  "kind": "command", "title": "Apply command"},
    {"op": "update", "id": "FR-001",     "fields": ["title", "body"]}
  ]
}

docs.title: Exit Codes
docs.text: Code Condition 0 Plan generated successfully 1 File not found or unreadable 2 Validation error in parsed content 5 Workspace not initialised

docs.title: speqlite plan
docs.text: Display the current pending plan from .spec/state.plan.json.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: speqlite plan [flags]

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Type Default Description --summary bool false Print only the count per operation type

docs.title: Output (text)
docs.text:
Pending plan (3 operations):

  + CMD-IMPORT   [command]     create   "Import command"
  ~ CMD-RENDER   [command]     update   title changed
  - CMD-OLD      [command]     delete   deprecated

Legend: + = create, ~ = update, - = delete/deprecate.

docs.title: Output (json)
docs.text: Same structure as the state.plan.json file. See Plan & Apply.

docs.title: Exit Codes
docs.text: Code Condition 0 Plan shown 3 No pending plan (state.plan.json absent or empty) 5 Workspace not initialised

docs.title: speqlite apply
docs.text: Apply the pending plan to .spec/state.sqlite. This is the only command that mutates canonical state.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: speqlite apply [flags]

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Type Default Description --auto-approve bool false Skip interactive confirmation prompt --dry-run bool false Show what would be applied; do not mutate state

docs.title: Behaviour
docs.text:
1. Reads .spec/state.plan.json.
  2. Validates the plan (schema check, conflict detection, transition validity).
  3. (If interactive) Prompts for confirmation.
  4. Executes all operations in a single SQLite transaction.
  5. Appends events to event_log for every operation.
  6. Updates state.snapshot.json to reflect new state.
  7. Removes state.plan.json.

docs.title: Output (text)
docs.text:
Applying 3 operations...

  ✓ create CMD-IMPORT
  ✓ create CMD-APPLY
  ✓ update CMD-RENDER

Applied successfully. State updated.

docs.title: Exit Codes
docs.text: Code Condition 0 All operations applied 2 Validation error; apply aborted 3 No pending plan 4 Conflict: version mismatch detected 5 Workspace not initialised

docs.title: speqlite render
docs.text: Generate Markdown projections from state.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: speqlite render [<spec-id>...] [flags]

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Type Default Description --all bool false Render all specs --output string specs/ Output directory for rendered files --format string markdown Output format: markdown, text, json --status string "" Only render specs with this status --kind string "" Only render specs of this kind

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
For each rendered spec, a file is created at {output}/{id}.md:

# CMD-IMPORT

**Kind**: command
**Status**: fixed
**Version**: 3
**Updated**: 2024-01-15T10:30:00Z

## Description

The import command reads Markdown or plain-text files...

## Relations

- depends_on: [STATE-SQLITE](STATE-SQLITE.md)
- implements: [FR-001](FR-001.md)

---
<!-- speqlite:id=CMD-IMPORT speqlite:version=3 speqlite:hash=abc123 -->

The HTML comment at the end is a roundtrip marker used by the importer to detect previously-rendered specs.

docs.title: Exit Codes
docs.text: Code Condition 0 All requested specs rendered 1 Output directory not writable 5 Workspace not initialised

docs.title: speqlite search
docs.text: Full-text search over spec titles and bodies using FTS5/BM25.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: speqlite search <query> [flags]

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Type Default Description --limit int 20 Maximum number of results --kind string "" Filter by spec kind --status string "" Filter by spec status --show-body bool false Include body excerpt in output

docs.title: Output (text)
docs.text:
CMD-APPLY      [command]     fixed       "Apply command"
CMD-PLAN       [command]     fixed       "Plan command"
FR-005         [requirement] implemented "Plan/apply workflow requirement"

docs.title: Output (json)
docs.text:
{
  "query": "plan apply",
  "results": [
    {"id": "CMD-APPLY", "title": "Apply command", "kind": "command", "status": "fixed", "rank": -1.23},
    {"id": "CMD-PLAN",  "title": "Plan command",  "kind": "command", "status": "fixed", "rank": -1.05}
  ]
}

docs.title: Exit Codes
docs.text: Code Condition 0 Search completed (zero results is still exit 0) 1 FTS table not available 5 Workspace not initialised

docs.title: speqlite deps
docs.text: Display the dependency graph for a spec node.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: speqlite deps <spec-id> [flags]

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Type Default Description --depth int 0 Max traversal depth (0 = unlimited) --direction string out Edge direction: out (what it depends on), in (what depends on it), both --relation string "" Filter by relation type

docs.title: Output (text)
docs.text:
CMD-APPLY
 ├── depends_on → CMD-PLAN
 │    └── depends_on → STATE-SQLITE
 └── depends_on → STATE-SQLITE

docs.title: Output (json)
docs.text:
{
  "root": "CMD-APPLY",
  "nodes": [
    {"id": "CMD-APPLY", "title": "Apply command", "depth": 0},
    {"id": "CMD-PLAN",  "title": "Plan command",  "depth": 1},
    {"id": "STATE-SQLITE", "title": "SQLite state", "depth": 1}
  ],
  "edges": [
    {"from": "CMD-APPLY", "relation": "depends_on", "to": "CMD-PLAN"},
    {"from": "CMD-APPLY", "relation": "depends_on", "to": "STATE-SQLITE"},
    {"from": "CMD-PLAN",  "relation": "depends_on", "to": "STATE-SQLITE"}
  ]
}

docs.title: Exit Codes
docs.text: Code Condition 0 Graph displayed 1 Spec ID not found 2 Cyclic dependency detected 5 Workspace not initialised

docs.title: speqlite validate
docs.text: Run structural and constraint validation against the current state.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: speqlite validate [flags]

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Type Default Description --fix bool false Auto-fix trivial issues (e.g. normalise IDs) --strict bool false Treat warnings as errors

docs.title: Checks Performed
docs.text: Check Error Code Severity Duplicate IDs E_DUPLICATE_ID Error Broken relation (target missing) E_BROKEN_RELATION Error Invalid status transition in plan E_INVALID_TRANSITION Error Cyclic dependency E_CYCLIC_DEP Error draft target in stable dependency E_DRAFT_DEPENDENCY Warning deprecated target in live dependency E_DEPRECATED_DEPENDENCY Warning Spec with no title E_MISSING_TITLE Warning Spec with no body E_EMPTY_BODY Warning (if --strict)

docs.title: Output (text)
docs.text:
Validation complete.

Errors (2):
  E_BROKEN_RELATION  CMD-APPLY depends_on MISSING-NODE (not found)
  E_CYCLIC_DEP       CMD-A → CMD-B → CMD-A

Warnings (1):
  E_DRAFT_DEPENDENCY  CMD-RENDER depends_on FR-002 (draft)

Exit code: 2

docs.title: Exit Codes
docs.text: Code Condition 0 No errors (warnings may exist) 2 One or more errors found 5 Workspace not initialised

docs.title: speqlite state
docs.text: Subcommand group for inspecting canonical state.

docs.title: speqlite state list
docs.text:
speqlite state list [flags]
Flag Description --status <s> Filter by status --kind <k> Filter by kind --sort <field> Sort by: id, title, updated_at, status (default: id)

Output:

ID             KIND          STATUS        TITLE
CMD-APPLY      command       fixed         Apply command
CMD-IMPORT     command       fixed         Import command
CMD-PLAN       command       fixed         Plan command
FR-001         requirement   implemented   Import functional requirement
STATE-SQLITE   state         verified      SQLite canonical state

docs.title: speqlite state show
docs.text:
speqlite state show <spec-id> [flags]

Prints full details of a single spec including body, relations, and constraints.

Output:

ID:         CMD-APPLY
Title:      Apply command
Kind:       command
Status:     fixed
Version:    3
Hash:       sha256:abc123...
Created:    2024-01-10T09:00:00Z
Updated:    2024-01-15T10:30:00Z

Body:
  The apply command commits the pending plan to state.sqlite...

Relations:
  depends_on → CMD-PLAN
  depends_on → STATE-SQLITE

Constraints: (none)

docs.title: speqlite state export
docs.text:
speqlite state export [flags]
Flag Description --format <f> Export format: json, snapshot (default: json) --output <path> Output file (default: stdout)

Exports the complete state as JSON. Equivalent to the content of state.snapshot.json.

docs.title: speqlite state transition
docs.text:
speqlite state transition <spec-id> --to <status> [flags]
Flag Description --to <status> Target status (required)

Generates a plan entry for the status transition. Run apply to commit.

docs.title: speqlite export
docs.text: Export specs to formal verification languages.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: speqlite export [flags]

docs.title: Flags
docs.text:
Flag Type Default Description --format string — Required. One of: alloy, smtlib, tla, lean, datalog --output string stdout Output file path --status string verified Only export specs with this status --include-all bool false Export all statuses

Note

Formal export stubs are included in the MVP binary but produce skeleton output only. Full expression generation is a post-MVP feature.

docs.title: speqlite version
docs.text:
Print version information.

speqlite version

Output:

speqlite v0.1.0 (commit: abc1234, built: 2024-01-15)

docs.title: Data Model
docs.text: Speclite's canonical state is an SQLite database located at .spec/state.sqlite. This page documents every table, column, constraint, index, and the FTS virtual table.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The schema contains five persistent objects:

Object Type Purpose specs Table Core specification nodes relations Table Typed edges between spec nodes constraints Table Formal expressions attached to specs event_log Table Immutable audit trail of all mutations specs_fts Virtual table (FTS5) Full-text search index

All timestamps are stored as ISO-8601 UTC strings (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ).

docs.title: Table: specs
docs.text:
Stores every specification node. Each row is one specification unit.

CREATE TABLE specs (
  id          TEXT    PRIMARY KEY,         -- canonical identifier, e.g. CMD-IMPORT
  title       TEXT    NOT NULL,            -- human-readable short title
  kind        TEXT    NOT NULL,            -- node type (see Kind Enumeration)
  status      TEXT    NOT NULL             -- lifecycle status (see State Machine)
              DEFAULT 'draft'
              CHECK (status IN ('draft','review','fixed','implemented','verified','deprecated')),
  version     INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,  -- monotonically increasing mutation counter
  body        TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '', -- full prose / Markdown body
  hash        TEXT    NOT NULL,            -- SHA-256 of canonical serialisation
  created_at  TEXT    NOT NULL,            -- ISO-8601 UTC creation timestamp
  updated_at  TEXT    NOT NULL             -- ISO-8601 UTC last-mutation timestamp
);

CREATE INDEX idx_specs_kind   ON specs(kind);
CREATE INDEX idx_specs_status ON specs(status);

docs.title: id
docs.text:
The canonical identifier for a specification node. IDs follow the pattern {PREFIX}-{SLUG} where:

  • PREFIX is an uppercase type abbreviation (e.g. CMD, FR, STATE, ARCH)
  • SLUG is an uppercase alphanumeric-plus-hyphen token (e.g. IMPORT, APPLY-01)

Examples: CMD-IMPORT, FR-001, STATE-SQLITE, ARCH-NORMALIZER

Rules: - Must be globally unique within a workspace - Must match the regex ^[A-Z][A-Z0-9]*(-[A-Z0-9]+)+$ - Immutable once created (rename = deprecate old + create new)

docs.title: title
docs.text: Short human-readable label. Used as the primary display string and as the weighted FTS field. Must not be empty.

docs.title: kind
docs.text:
Classifies the node's semantic role. The normalizer uses kind to infer which rendering template and validation rules apply.

Value Meaning requirement Functional or non-functional requirement command CLI command specification architecture Architectural decision or component description data-model Schema, type, or data structure definition state A named state or lifecycle node constraint A formal or semi-formal constraint expression test A test case or acceptance criterion glossary Term definition note Informal note or context

The set is extensible; unknown kinds are accepted and stored as-is.

docs.title: status
docs.text:
Lifecycle state of the specification. See State Machine for the full transition table.

Value Meaning draft Initial state; under authoring review Submitted for review; no further edits without state transition fixed Agreed upon; no further changes expected implemented Corresponding code/artefact exists verified Independently verified by test or audit deprecated Superseded or removed; retained for history

docs.title: version
docs.text: Monotonically increasing integer. Starts at 1 on creation. Incremented by 1 on every update_spec event. Used for optimistic concurrency detection during plan/apply.

docs.title: body
docs.text: Full prose or Markdown content of the specification. May be empty for stub nodes. Stored verbatim; rendering is handled by the renderer package.

docs.title: hash
docs.text:
SHA-256 hex digest of the canonical serialisation of the spec node. The canonical serialisation is:

{id}
{title}
{kind}
{status}
{body}

Used by the planner to detect content changes without full body comparison.

docs.title: created_at / updated_at
docs.text: ISO-8601 UTC timestamps. created_at is set once at row creation and never updated. updated_at is refreshed on every mutation.

docs.title: Table: relations
docs.text:
Stores directed, typed edges between spec nodes.

CREATE TABLE relations (
  from_id   TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES specs(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  relation  TEXT NOT NULL
            CHECK (relation IN ('depends_on','implements','verifies','supersedes','related_to')),
  to_id     TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES specs(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
  PRIMARY KEY (from_id, relation, to_id)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_relations_to_id ON relations(to_id);

docs.title: Relation Types
docs.text: Relation Direction Meaning depends_on from requires to from cannot be implemented without to implements from provides to from is the concrete realisation of to verifies from checks to from is a test/audit for to supersedes from replaces to from is the newer version that obsoletes to related_to bidirectional (by convention) Loose thematic relationship

docs.title: Constraints
docs.text:
• ON DELETE CASCADE on from_id: deleting a spec removes all its outgoing relations.
  • ON DELETE RESTRICT on to_id: a spec cannot be deleted if other specs depend on it (prevents broken relations). To remove such a spec, first remove or reroute the incoming relations.
  • The composite primary key prevents duplicate edges of the same type.
  • Self-referencing edges (from_id = to_id) are forbidden at the application layer.

docs.title: Table: constraints
docs.text:
Stores formal or semi-formal constraint expressions attached to spec nodes. Designed for future export to formal verification tools.

CREATE TABLE constraints (
  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,           -- unique constraint identifier
  target_id   TEXT NOT NULL               -- spec node this constraint applies to
              REFERENCES specs(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  language    TEXT NOT NULL,             -- expression language (see below)
  expression  TEXT NOT NULL,             -- the constraint expression
  created_at  TEXT NOT NULL              -- ISO-8601 UTC
);

CREATE INDEX idx_constraints_target ON constraints(target_id);

docs.title: Expression Languages
docs.text:
Value Toolchain Notes natural None Free-form English invariant; not machine-checked alloy Alloy Analyser Relational logic smtlib Z3, CVC5 SMT-LIB 2.x tla TLC model checker TLA+ lean Lean 4 Dependent type theory datalog Soufflé, µZ Deductive rules

All non-natural languages are stored verbatim for future export; the MVP does not execute them.

docs.title: Table: event_log
docs.text:
Append-only audit trail. No row is ever updated or deleted. Provides full history of all state mutations.

CREATE TABLE event_log (
  id           INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  event_type   TEXT    NOT NULL,          -- see Event Types below
  spec_id      TEXT,                      -- may be NULL for workspace-level events
  payload_json TEXT    NOT NULL           -- JSON blob, schema varies by event_type
               DEFAULT '{}',
  created_at   TEXT    NOT NULL           -- ISO-8601 UTC
);

CREATE INDEX idx_event_log_spec_id    ON event_log(spec_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_event_log_event_type ON event_log(event_type);
CREATE INDEX idx_event_log_created_at ON event_log(created_at);

docs.title: Event Types
docs.text: event_type Trigger payload_json fields create_spec New spec node added via apply {id, title, kind, status} update_spec Existing spec body or title mutated {id, old_hash, new_hash, fields_changed:[...]} change_status Spec lifecycle transition {id, from_status, to_status} create_relation New edge added {from_id, relation, to_id} remove_relation Edge removed {from_id, relation, to_id} create_constraint Formal constraint added {id, target_id, language} apply_plan A plan file was applied {plan_hash, ops_count} init_workspace Workspace initialised {version}

docs.title: Virtual Table: specs_fts
docs.text:
Full-text search index powered by SQLite FTS5 with BM25 ranking.

CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE specs_fts USING fts5(
  id    UNINDEXED,   -- stored but not tokenised
  title,             -- weighted 3× via rank() bm25 boost
  body,              -- full body text
  content='specs',   -- shadow table: keeps FTS in sync with specs
  content_rowid='rowid'
);

-- Triggers to keep FTS in sync with specs
CREATE TRIGGER specs_ai AFTER INSERT ON specs BEGIN
  INSERT INTO specs_fts(rowid, id, title, body)
  VALUES (new.rowid, new.id, new.title, new.body);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER specs_ad AFTER DELETE ON specs BEGIN
  INSERT INTO specs_fts(specs_fts, rowid, id, title, body)
  VALUES ('delete', old.rowid, old.id, old.title, old.body);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER specs_au AFTER UPDATE ON specs BEGIN
  INSERT INTO specs_fts(specs_fts, rowid, id, title, body)
  VALUES ('delete', old.rowid, old.id, old.title, old.body);
  INSERT INTO specs_fts(rowid, id, title, body)
  VALUES (new.rowid, new.id, new.title, new.body);
END;

docs.title: Search Query
docs.text:
SELECT
  s.id,
  s.title,
  s.kind,
  s.status,
  bm25(specs_fts, 0, 3.0, 1.0) AS rank   -- title weighted 3×
FROM specs_fts
JOIN specs s ON s.id = specs_fts.id
WHERE specs_fts MATCH ?
  AND s.kind   = ?   -- optional kind filter
  AND s.status = ?   -- optional status filter
ORDER BY rank
LIMIT ?;

The bm25() auxiliary function's column weights are: - Column 0 (id): 0 (UNINDEXED, not ranked) - Column 1 (title): 3.0 (title match boosted) - Column 2 (body): 1.0 (baseline)

docs.title: Entity-Relationship Diagram
docs.text:
┌───────────┐      ┌───────────────┐      ┌───────────┐
│   specs   │◄─────│   relations   │─────►│   specs   │
│           │      │  from_id      │      │           │
│  id (PK)  │      │  relation     │      │  id (PK)  │
│  title    │      │  to_id        │      │  ...      │
│  kind     │      └───────────────┘      └───────────┘
│  status   │
│  version  │      ┌───────────────┐
│  body     │◄─────│  constraints  │
│  hash     │      │  id (PK)      │
│  ...      │      │  target_id    │
└───────────┘      │  language     │
      │            │  expression   │
      │            └───────────────┘
      │
      ▼
┌───────────────┐      ┌─────────────┐
│   event_log   │      │  specs_fts  │
│  id (PK auto) │      │  (virtual)  │
│  event_type   │      │  id         │
│  spec_id      │      │  title      │
│  payload_json │      │  body       │
│  created_at   │      └─────────────┘
└───────────────┘

docs.title: Schema Version & Migration
docs.text:
The schema version is stored in the SQLite user_version pragma:

PRAGMA user_version = 1;

Migrations are applied sequentially. Each migration file is named NNN_description.sql. The apply engine checks user_version at startup and runs pending migrations before any operation.

docs.title: Full DDL (Single Block)
docs.text:
PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;
PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
PRAGMA user_version = 1;

CREATE TABLE specs (
  id          TEXT    PRIMARY KEY,
  title       TEXT    NOT NULL,
  kind        TEXT    NOT NULL,
  status      TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft'
              CHECK (status IN ('draft','review','fixed','implemented','verified','deprecated')),
  version     INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
  body        TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  hash        TEXT    NOT NULL,
  created_at  TEXT    NOT NULL,
  updated_at  TEXT    NOT NULL
);

CREATE INDEX idx_specs_kind   ON specs(kind);
CREATE INDEX idx_specs_status ON specs(status);

CREATE TABLE relations (
  from_id   TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES specs(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  relation  TEXT NOT NULL
            CHECK (relation IN ('depends_on','implements','verifies','supersedes','related_to')),
  to_id     TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES specs(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
  PRIMARY KEY (from_id, relation, to_id)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_relations_to_id ON relations(to_id);

CREATE TABLE constraints (
  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  target_id   TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES specs(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  language    TEXT NOT NULL,
  expression  TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at  TEXT NOT NULL
);

CREATE INDEX idx_constraints_target ON constraints(target_id);

CREATE TABLE event_log (
  id           INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  event_type   TEXT    NOT NULL,
  spec_id      TEXT,
  payload_json TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
  created_at   TEXT    NOT NULL
);

CREATE INDEX idx_event_log_spec_id    ON event_log(spec_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_event_log_event_type ON event_log(event_type);
CREATE INDEX idx_event_log_created_at ON event_log(created_at);

CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE specs_fts USING fts5(
  id    UNINDEXED,
  title,
  body,
  content='specs',
  content_rowid='rowid'
);

CREATE TRIGGER specs_ai AFTER INSERT ON specs BEGIN
  INSERT INTO specs_fts(rowid, id, title, body)
  VALUES (new.rowid, new.id, new.title, new.body);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER specs_ad AFTER DELETE ON specs BEGIN
  INSERT INTO specs_fts(specs_fts, rowid, id, title, body)
  VALUES ('delete', old.rowid, old.id, old.title, old.body);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER specs_au AFTER UPDATE ON specs BEGIN
  INSERT INTO specs_fts(specs_fts, rowid, id, title, body)
  VALUES ('delete', old.rowid, old.id, old.title, old.body);
  INSERT INTO specs_fts(rowid, id, title, body)
  VALUES (new.rowid, new.id, new.title, new.body);
END;

docs.title: File Structure
docs.text: This page documents the directory layout of a Speclite workspace and the purpose of every file and directory.

docs.title: Workspace Root Layout
docs.text:
{project-root}/
│
├── .spec/                   ← Speclite state directory (managed by speqlite)
│   ├── state.sqlite         ← Canonical state database
│   ├── state.snapshot.json  ← Point-in-time snapshot of state (updated on apply)
│   └── state.plan.json      ← Pending plan (written by import, consumed by apply)
│
├── specs/                   ← Rendered Markdown projections (regenerated by render)
│   ├── CMD-IMPORT.md
│   ├── CMD-APPLY.md
│   └── FR-001.md
│
├── scratch/                 ← Working area for rough input files
│   ├── feature-ideas.md
│   └── ai-generated.md
│
└── changes/                 ← Archived plan files after apply (optional, for audit)
    ├── 20240115-apply.json
    └── 20240116-apply.json

docs.title: .spec/ Directory
docs.text: The .spec/ directory is managed exclusively by speqlite. Do not edit files in this directory manually.

docs.title: .spec/state.sqlite
docs.text:
The canonical state database. Contains all spec nodes, relations, constraints, and event history.

  • Created by speqlite init.
  • Read by all commands.
  • Written only by speqlite apply.
  • Should be committed to Git for team sharing.
  • Uses SQLite WAL mode for safe concurrent reads.

Git recommendation: Commit this file. SQLite WAL mode produces -wal and -shm files during active writes; add them to .gitignore.

.spec/state.sqlite-wal
.spec/state.sqlite-shm

docs.title: .spec/state.snapshot.json
docs.text:
A JSON snapshot of the metadata of all specs (no body text). Updated atomically by speqlite apply.

Purpose: - Enables the planner to compute diffs without loading the full SQLite database. - Is human-readable and Git-diffable. - Used for conflict detection during apply.

Git recommendation: Commit this file. Review its diff in pull requests to see what specs changed.

Example: 

{
  "version": 1,
  "snapshot_hash": "sha256:cafebabe...",
  "taken_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "specs": [
    {"id": "CMD-IMPORT", "title": "Import command", "kind": "command", "status": "fixed", "version": 3, "hash": "sha256:abc123...", "updated_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"}
  ],
  "relations": [
    {"from_id": "CMD-IMPORT", "relation": "depends_on", "to_id": "STATE-SQLITE"}
  ],
  "constraints": []
}

docs.title: .spec/state.plan.json
docs.text:
The pending plan, written by speqlite import and consumed by speqlite apply. Deleted after successful apply.

Git recommendation: Do not commit this file (add to .gitignore). Plans are ephemeral working state.

.spec/state.plan.json

If you want to preserve plans for audit, use the changes/ directory (see below).

docs.title: specs/ Directory
docs.text: Contains Markdown projections rendered by speqlite render. These files are derived from state and can always be regenerated.

docs.title: File Naming
docs.text:
{spec-id}.md

Examples: CMD-IMPORT.md, FR-001.md, STATE-SQLITE.md

docs.title: File Format
docs.text:
# CMD-IMPORT: Import Command

**Kind**: command
**Status**: fixed
**Version**: 3
**Updated**: 2024-01-15T10:30:00Z

## Description

The import command reads Markdown or plain-text files...

## Relations

- depends_on: [STATE-SQLITE](STATE-SQLITE.md)
- implements: [FR-001](FR-001.md)

---
<!-- speqlite:id=CMD-IMPORT speqlite:version=3 speqlite:hash=sha256:abc123 -->

The HTML comment at the end is the roundtrip marker. The normalizer uses it to match rendered files back to their canonical IDs without relying on filenames.

Git recommendation: Commit these files. They serve as human- and AI-readable documentation.

docs.title: scratch/ Directory
docs.text:
A working area for rough input files. Users write informal Markdown or plain text here, then import it.

Convention: - Files in scratch/ are not managed by Speclite. - They are free-form; the normalizer handles them. - After import and apply, scratch files can be archived or deleted.

Git recommendation: Optional. Some teams commit scratch files as a record of intent; others .gitignore them.

docs.title: changes/ Directory
docs.text:
An optional directory for archiving applied plans. After speqlite apply succeeds, users can move (or configure Speclite to copy) state.plan.json here with a timestamp prefix.

changes/
  20240115T103000Z-apply.json
  20240116T082000Z-apply.json

This provides a full audit trail of every state mutation, even beyond what the event log records (which stays in SQLite).

Git recommendation: Commit if you want a Git-tracked audit trail outside SQLite.

docs.title: Recommended .gitignore
docs.text:
# Speclite ephemeral files
.spec/state.plan.json
.spec/state.sqlite-wal
.spec/state.sqlite-shm

# Optional: ignore scratch if not tracking input files
# scratch/

docs.title: File Ownership Summary
docs.text: Path Managed by Git .spec/state.sqlite speqlite apply only Commit .spec/state.snapshot.json speqlite apply only Commit .spec/state.plan.json speqlite import / apply Ignore .spec/state.sqlite-wal SQLite Ignore .spec/state.sqlite-shm SQLite Ignore specs/*.md speqlite render Commit scratch/* User Optional changes/*.json User / scripts Optional

docs.title: Multi-Repository Workspaces
docs.text:
Speclite is designed for single-repository use in the MVP. If you have multiple repositories, run speqlite init in each and use speqlite state export to exchange snapshots.

Cross-repository relation tracking (where from_id and to_id live in different workspaces) is a post-MVP feature.

docs.title: Environment Variable Overrides
docs.text:
Variable Default Description SPECLITE_WORKSPACE . Workspace root directory SPECLITE_DB_PATH .spec/state.sqlite Path to SQLite database SPECLITE_PLAN_PATH .spec/state.plan.json Path to plan file SPECLITE_SNAPSHOT_PATH .spec/state.snapshot.json Path to snapshot file SPECLITE_SPECS_DIR specs/ Directory for rendered Markdown SPECLITE_SCRATCH_DIR scratch/ Directory for input files

All environment variables can be overridden by the corresponding CLI flag (--workspace, --db-path, etc.).

docs.title: Future Extensions
docs.text: This page documents the planned extension points for Speclite beyond the MVP. These are first-class architectural concerns — the data model and state machine are designed to support them — but they are not implemented in v0.1.

docs.title: MCP Server
docs.text: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) allows AI assistants to call structured tools to retrieve context. A Speclite MCP server exposes spec state as an AI-readable API without requiring the AI to run CLI commands or parse raw Markdown.

docs.title: Motivation
docs.text:
In an AI-native workflow, the LLM needs: - Access to the latest stable specifications - The ability to search by topic - The ability to traverse dependency graphs - A way to check if a proposed change would violate constraints

The MCP server provides all of this as structured function calls.

docs.title: spec_get
docs.text:
Retrieve a single spec node by ID.

{
  "name": "spec_get",
  "description": "Get a single specification by ID",
  "inputSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "id": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "Spec ID, e.g. CMD-IMPORT"
      },
      "include_body": {
        "type": "boolean",
        "default": true,
        "description": "Include full body text in response"
      }
    },
    "required": ["id"]
  }
}

Response: 

{
  "id": "CMD-IMPORT",
  "title": "Import command",
  "kind": "command",
  "status": "fixed",
  "version": 3,
  "body": "The import command reads...",
  "relations": [
    {"relation": "depends_on", "to_id": "STATE-SQLITE"},
    {"relation": "implements", "to_id": "FR-001"}
  ]
}

docs.title: spec_search
docs.text:
Full-text search over specs using FTS5/BM25.

{
  "name": "spec_search",
  "description": "Search specs by keyword",
  "inputSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "query": {"type": "string"},
      "limit": {"type": "integer", "default": 10},
      "kind": {"type": "string"},
      "status": {"type": "string"}
    },
    "required": ["query"]
  }
}

docs.title: spec_deps
docs.text:
Traverse the dependency graph from a root node.

{
  "name": "spec_deps",
  "description": "Get the dependency graph for a spec",
  "inputSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "id": {"type": "string"},
      "direction": {"type": "string", "enum": ["out", "in", "both"], "default": "out"},
      "depth": {"type": "integer", "default": 3},
      "relation": {"type": "string"}
    },
    "required": ["id"]
  }
}

docs.title: spec_validate
docs.text:
Run validation checks and return structured errors.

{
  "name": "spec_validate",
  "description": "Validate the current spec state",
  "inputSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "spec_id": {"type": "string", "description": "Validate only this spec; omit for full workspace"},
      "strict": {"type": "boolean", "default": false}
    }
  }
}

docs.title: spec_list
docs.text:
List specs matching filters.

{
  "name": "spec_list",
  "description": "List specs with optional filtering",
  "inputSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "kind": {"type": "string"},
      "status": {"type": "string"},
      "limit": {"type": "integer", "default": 50}
    }
  }
}

docs.title: MCP Server Architecture
docs.text:
speqlite mcp serve [--port 3000] [--transport stdio|sse]
  • Default transport: stdio (for use with Claude Desktop, Cursor, etc.)
  • Optional transport: sse (Server-Sent Events for HTTP clients)
  • Reads from state.sqlite in read-only mode (no mutations via MCP in v1)
  • Authentication: none in MVP (local-only); API key in future versions

docs.title: Implementation Notes
docs.text:
• Use the mark3labs/mcp-go library for the Go implementation.
  • The MCP server is a separate binary target: cmd/speqlite-mcp/main.go.
  • It shares the internal/db and internal/search packages with the CLI.

docs.title: Formal Language Export
docs.text: The constraints table stores formal expressions for future export to verification tools. The export pipeline emits these expressions in tool-specific syntax.

docs.title: Alloy
docs.text:
Alloy is a relational modelling language for structural invariants.

speqlite export --format alloy --output spec.als

MVP output (stub): 

-- Generated by speqlite export --format alloy
-- Workspace: my-project
-- Date: 2024-01-15

module Speclite

sig Spec {
  id: one String,
  kind: one Kind,
  status: one Status,
  depends_on: set Spec,
  implements: set Spec
}

enum Kind { command, requirement, architecture, state, constraint, test, glossary, note }
enum Status { draft, review, fixed, implemented, verified, deprecated }

-- Constraints from workspace:
-- (full expression generation is post-MVP)

docs.title: SMT-LIB 2
docs.text:
For constraint solving with Z3 or CVC5.

speqlite export --format smtlib --output spec.smt2

MVP output (stub): 

; Generated by speqlite export --format smtlib
; Workspace: my-project

(declare-sort Spec 0)
(declare-fun id (Spec) String)
(declare-fun kind (Spec) String)
(declare-fun status (Spec) String)
(declare-fun depends_on (Spec Spec) Bool)

; Constraints: (post-MVP)

docs.title: TLA+
docs.text:
For model checking with TLC.

speqlite export --format tla --output spec.tla

MVP output (stub): 

---- MODULE Speclite ----
EXTENDS Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANTS SpecIDs

VARIABLES status

TypeInvariant ==
  \A id \in SpecIDs :
    status[id] \in {"draft","review","fixed","implemented","verified","deprecated"}

====

docs.title: Lean 4
docs.text:
For theorem proving.

speqlite export --format lean --output Spec.lean

docs.title: Datalog
docs.text:
For deductive reasoning with Soufflé or µZ.

speqlite export --format datalog --output spec.dl

docs.title: Export Pipeline Architecture
docs.text:
internal/exporter/
  alloy.go      -- Alloy .als generator
  smtlib.go     -- SMT-LIB 2 .smt2 generator
  tla.go        -- TLA+ .tla generator
  lean.go       -- Lean 4 .lean generator
  datalog.go    -- Datalog .dl generator
  exporter.go   -- common interface: Exporter.Export([]SpecNode, io.Writer) error

The Exporter interface:

type Exporter interface {
    Format() string
    Export(nodes []db.Spec, relations []db.Relation, constraints []db.Constraint, w io.Writer) error
}

docs.title: Constraint Expression Language (Future)
docs.text:
In a future version, constraints will be authored in a Speclite DSL that compiles to multiple targets:

CONSTRAINT CONSTR-001 ON FR-001:
  INVARIANT: all specs in status "implemented" have at least one "verifies" relation

This DSL is not part of the MVP. MVP constraints are stored as raw strings in the target language and emitted verbatim.

docs.title: Web UI (non-goal until v2)
docs.text: A local web server (speqlite ui) serving a React SPA that visualises the spec graph. Not in scope for v1.

docs.title: Watch Mode
docs.text:
speqlite watch scratch/

Automatically re-imports changed files and updates the pending plan. Implemented as a file-system watcher loop.

docs.title: CI Integration
docs.text:
speqlite validate --exit-on-error

Returns exit code 2 on validation errors, suitable for use in CI pipelines. This is already part of the MVP CLI but documented here for CI-specific usage patterns.

docs.title: Spec Linting
docs.text:
Custom lint rules defined in .spec/lint.yaml:

rules:
  - id: no-draft-fixed-dependency
    level: error
  - id: require-body-for-fixed
    level: warning
  - id: max-depth
    value: 5
    level: warning

docs.title: Embedding / Semantic Search
docs.text: Integration with a local embedding model (e.g., nomic-embed-text via Ollama) to provide semantic search alongside BM25. Requires a vector storage extension — post-MVP.

docs.title: Normalizer Algorithm
docs.text: The normalizer converts unstructured Markdown or plain text into a list of SpecNode values that can be diffed against the current state. It is the core of the AI-friendly import pipeline.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Input file (Markdown / plain text)
  │
  ▼
┌─────────────┐
│   Importer  │   Detect file type, split into raw sections
└──────┬──────┘
       │  []RawSection
       ▼
┌──────────────┐
│   Normalizer │   Extract IDs, titles, kinds, relations, body
└──────┬───────┘
       │  []SpecNode
       ▼
┌─────────────┐
│   Planner   │   Diff against state snapshot → Plan ops
└─────────────┘

The normalizer is idempotent: running it twice on the same input produces the same output.

docs.title: Phase 1: Section Splitting
docs.text: The importer splits the input into raw sections. A section is a contiguous block of text that is expected to represent a single spec node.

docs.title: Markdown Splitting Rules
docs.text:
The Goldmark parser is used to parse the Markdown AST. Section boundaries are determined as follows:

  1. Heading-based splitting: Each heading at the configured split level (default: H2) starts a new section. The heading text becomes the section title candidate.
  2. Horizontal rule splitting: A --- or *** at the top level can optionally split sections (disabled by default; enabled with --split-on-hr).
  3. Front matter: YAML front matter (between --- delimiters at the top of the file) is parsed separately and applies to the first section or to all sections if it contains a global: true key.

docs.title: Example Markdown
docs.text:
# My Feature Spec

This is the overview.

## CMD-IMPORT: Import Command

Reads Markdown files and generates a plan.

**Depends on**: STATE-SQLITE, FR-001

## FR-001: Import Functional Requirement

The system must support importing Markdown files.

This is split into 3 sections: - Section 0: # My Feature Spec (file-level, becomes a note kind if no ID detected) - Section 1: ## CMD-IMPORT: Import Command - Section 2: ## FR-001: Import Functional Requirement

docs.title: Plain Text Splitting Rules
docs.text:
For non-Markdown plain text:

  1. Blank-line block splitting: Consecutive non-blank lines form a block. Two or more blank lines end a block.
  2. ID-line detection: If the first line of a block matches the ID pattern, it is treated as a section header.
  3. If no sections can be determined, the entire file is treated as one section.

docs.title: Phase 2: ID Extraction
docs.text: For each raw section, the normalizer attempts to extract or generate a canonical ID.

docs.title: Heuristic Priority Order
docs.text: The normalizer tries each heuristic in order and uses the first match:

docs.title: Heuristic H1: Explicit ID in Heading
docs.text:
Pattern: heading text matches ^({ID_PATTERN})[:\s-]+(.+)$

ID_PATTERN = [A-Z][A-Z0-9]*(-[A-Z0-9]+)+

Examples: - ## CMD-IMPORT: Import Command → ID = CMD-IMPORT, title = Import Command - ## FR-001 - Functional Requirement → ID = FR-001, title = Functional Requirement - ## STATE-SQLITE → ID = STATE-SQLITE, title = STATE-SQLITE (title = ID if no suffix)

docs.title: Heuristic H2: ID in First Body Line
docs.text:
If the heading does not contain an ID, check the first non-empty line of the body:

CMD-IMPORT: Import Command

Pattern: ^({ID_PATTERN})[:\s]+(.+)$ or ^({ID_PATTERN})$

docs.title: Heuristic H3: Roundtrip Marker
docs.text:
If the document was previously rendered by speqlite render, it contains a roundtrip marker:

<!-- speqlite:id=CMD-IMPORT speqlite:version=3 speqlite:hash=abc123 -->

This is parsed as the authoritative ID and version.

docs.title: Heuristic H4: ID Generation
docs.text:
If no ID is found, the normalizer generates one:

generated_id = {PREFIX}-{SLUG}

Where: - PREFIX is derived from the inferred kind (see Phase 4): CMD, FR, ARCH, STATE, NOTE, etc. - SLUG is generated from the title using: 1. Convert to uppercase 2. Replace non-alphanumeric with - 3. Trim leading/trailing - 4. Truncate to 20 characters 5. Append a 3-digit sequence number if collision detected: CMD-IMPORT-001

docs.title: Heuristic H5: Prefix Override
docs.text: If --id-prefix is passed on the CLI, all generated IDs use that prefix regardless of inferred kind.

docs.title: Phase 3: Title Inference
docs.text:
If the ID was extracted from the heading (H1), the title is the remainder of the heading text after the ID and separator.

If the ID came from the body or was generated (H2–H5), the title is:

  1. The first heading in the section, if present, with the ID removed.
  2. The first sentence of the body (up to the first ., !, or ?, maximum 80 characters).
  3. If nothing is found: the ID itself.

Title normalisation: - Strip leading/trailing whitespace. - Collapse internal whitespace to a single space. - Strip surrounding backticks, asterisks, and quotes.

docs.title: Phase 4: Kind Classification
docs.text: The normalizer assigns a kind to each section using a rule-based classifier.

docs.title: Classification Rules (in priority order)
docs.text: Rule Pattern Assigned Kind Front matter kind: field kind: command Verbatim value Explicit roundtrip marker speqlite:kind speqlite:kind=command Verbatim value --kind CLI flag — Verbatim value ID prefix CMD- CMD-* command ID prefix FR- FR-* requirement ID prefix ARCH- ARCH-* architecture ID prefix STATE- STATE-* state ID prefix TEST- TEST-* test ID prefix CONSTR- CONSTR-* constraint ID prefix GLOSS- GLOSS-* glossary Heading keywords heading contains Requirement, Must, Shall requirement Heading keywords heading contains Command, CLI, Flag command Heading keywords heading contains Architecture, Design, Package architecture Heading keywords heading contains State, Status, Lifecycle state Default — note

docs.title: Phase 5: Relation Parsing
docs.text: The normalizer scans the body of each section for relation declarations.

docs.title: Inline Relation Syntax
docs.text:
Recognised patterns (case-insensitive):

**Depends on**: CMD-PLAN, STATE-SQLITE
**Implements**: FR-001
**Verifies**: FR-003
**Supersedes**: CMD-IMPORT-OLD
**Related to**: ARCH-NORMALIZER

Also recognised: 

- depends_on: CMD-PLAN
- implements: FR-001

And YAML front matter: 

relations:
  depends_on: [CMD-PLAN, STATE-SQLITE]
  implements: [FR-001]

docs.title: Parsed Relation Object
docs.text:
type Relation struct {
    FromID   string
    Relation string  // one of: depends_on, implements, verifies, supersedes, related_to
    ToID     string
}

Relations referencing unknown IDs are preserved in the plan as pending relations. The validator will flag them as E_BROKEN_RELATION unless the target is also being created in the same plan.

docs.title: Phase 6: Body Cleaning
docs.text:
After extracting metadata (ID, title, kind, relations), the normalizer produces a cleaned body:

  1. Remove the first heading line (used as title).
  2. Remove any roundtrip marker HTML comment.
  3. Remove relation declaration lines (the metadata is stored in relations table separately).
  4. Remove YAML front matter block.
  5. Strip leading/trailing blank lines.
  6. Normalise line endings to \n.

The resulting body is stored verbatim in specs.body.

docs.title: Phase 7: Hash Computation
docs.text:
A SHA-256 hash is computed over the canonical serialisation:

{id}
{title}
{kind}
{status}
{body}

Note: status is included so that a status transition changes the hash and triggers an update event.

docs.title: Full Normalizer Pseudocode
docs.text:
func Normalize(file File, opts Options) ([]SpecNode, error):
    raw = parse(file)                         // Phase 1: split
    sections = splitSections(raw, opts)

    nodes = []
    for section in sections:
        id, title = extractID(section, opts)  // Phase 2 & 3
        if id == "":
            id = generateID(title, opts)

        kind = classifyKind(id, section, opts) // Phase 4

        relations = parseRelations(section)    // Phase 5

        body = cleanBody(section, id, title)   // Phase 6

        hash = sha256(id + title + kind + status + body) // Phase 7

        nodes = append(nodes, SpecNode{
            ID:        id,
            Title:     title,
            Kind:      kind,
            Status:    opts.DefaultStatus,  // "draft"
            Body:      body,
            Hash:      hash,
            Relations: relations,
        })

    return deduplicate(nodes), nil

docs.title: Deduplication
docs.text:
If the same ID appears in multiple sections (e.g., the same ID in two files), the normalizer:

  1. Merges the bodies (appends later body to first body with a separator).
  2. Merges relation sets (union).
  3. Logs a warning: W_DUPLICATE_ID_MERGED.

docs.title: Error and Warning Codes
docs.text: Code Level Description W_DUPLICATE_ID_MERGED Warning Same ID found in multiple sections; bodies merged W_ID_GENERATED Info ID was generated (not explicitly declared) W_KIND_DEFAULTED Info Kind defaulted to note W_EMPTY_BODY Warning Section has no body after cleaning E_INVALID_ID_FORMAT Error Generated ID does not match ID_PATTERN E_RELATION_PARSE Warning Relation line found but could not be fully parsed

docs.title: Plan & Apply
docs.text: Speclite follows a Terraform-inspired plan/apply workflow. No mutation of canonical state occurs without a reviewable plan.

docs.title: Workflow Overview
docs.text:
speqlite import scratch.md
  │
  ▼ writes
.spec/state.plan.json
  │
  ▼ reviewed by
speqlite plan
  │
  ▼ committed by
speqlite apply
  │
  ▼ updates
.spec/state.sqlite
.spec/state.snapshot.json
  │  removes
.spec/state.plan.json

docs.title: state.plan.json — JSON Schema
docs.text: The plan file is a JSON document describing a set of ordered operations to be applied atomically.

docs.title: Top-Level Schema
docs.text:
{
  "$schema": "https://speqlite.dev/schemas/plan/v1.json",
  "version": 1,
  "plan_hash": "<sha256 of canonical plan serialisation>",
  "generated_at": "<ISO-8601 UTC>",
  "source_files": ["scratch/feature.md"],
  "snapshot_hash": "<hash of state.snapshot.json at plan time>",
  "ops": [ ... ]
}
Field Type Description version integer Schema version; currently 1 plan_hash string SHA-256 of ops array serialised as compact JSON generated_at string ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of plan generation source_files string[] Files that were imported to produce this plan snapshot_hash string Hash of state.snapshot.json at the time the plan was created. Used for conflict detection at apply time. ops Op[] Ordered list of operations

docs.title: Operation Object
docs.text:
Each element of ops is an operation:

{
  "op": "create" | "update" | "delete" | "transition",
  "id": "<spec-id>",
  ...op-specific fields...
}

docs.title: create Operation
docs.text:
Creates a new spec node.

{
  "op": "create",
  "id": "CMD-IMPORT",
  "title": "Import command",
  "kind": "command",
  "status": "draft",
  "body": "The import command reads...",
  "hash": "sha256:abc123...",
  "relations": [
    {"relation": "depends_on", "to_id": "STATE-SQLITE"}
  ]
}
Field Type Required Description op string yes "create" id string yes New spec ID title string yes Spec title kind string yes Spec kind status string yes Initial status (usually "draft") body string yes Full body text hash string yes SHA-256 of canonical serialisation relations Relation[] no Relations to create alongside the spec

docs.title: update Operation
docs.text:
Updates an existing spec node's mutable fields.

{
  "op": "update",
  "id": "CMD-RENDER",
  "expected_version": 2,
  "expected_hash": "sha256:old123...",
  "fields": {
    "title": "Render command",
    "body": "Updated body text...",
    "hash": "sha256:new456..."
  }
}
Field Type Required Description op string yes "update" id string yes Spec ID to update expected_version integer yes Version at plan time; used for conflict detection expected_hash string yes Hash at plan time; used for conflict detection fields object yes Fields to update (only changed fields included)

Updatable fields: title, body, hash. The kind and id are immutable.

docs.title: delete Operation
docs.text:
Marks a spec as deprecated (hard delete is not supported in MVP).

{
  "op": "delete",
  "id": "CMD-OLD",
  "expected_version": 1,
  "mode": "deprecate"
}
Field Type Required Description op string yes "delete" id string yes Spec ID to deprecate expected_version integer yes Version at plan time mode string yes Currently only "deprecate"

docs.title: transition Operation
docs.text:
Transitions a spec to a new lifecycle status.

{
  "op": "transition",
  "id": "CMD-IMPORT",
  "from_status": "draft",
  "to_status": "review",
  "expected_version": 1
}
Field Type Required Description op string yes "transition" id string yes Spec ID from_status string yes Expected current status (for conflict detection) to_status string yes Target status expected_version integer yes Version at plan time

docs.title: add_relation Operation
docs.text:
Adds a relation edge.

{
  "op": "add_relation",
  "from_id": "CMD-APPLY",
  "relation": "depends_on",
  "to_id": "CMD-PLAN"
}

docs.title: remove_relation Operation
docs.text:
Removes a relation edge.

{
  "op": "remove_relation",
  "from_id": "CMD-APPLY",
  "relation": "depends_on",
  "to_id": "CMD-OLD"
}

docs.title: Complete state.plan.json Example
docs.text:
{
  "$schema": "https://speqlite.dev/schemas/plan/v1.json",
  "version": 1,
  "plan_hash": "sha256:deadbeef...",
  "generated_at": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z",
  "source_files": ["scratch/feature.md"],
  "snapshot_hash": "sha256:cafebabe...",
  "ops": [
    {
      "op": "create",
      "id": "CMD-IMPORT",
      "title": "Import command",
      "kind": "command",
      "status": "draft",
      "body": "The import command reads Markdown or plain-text files...",
      "hash": "sha256:abc111...",
      "relations": [
        {"relation": "depends_on", "to_id": "STATE-SQLITE"},
        {"relation": "implements", "to_id": "FR-001"}
      ]
    },
    {
      "op": "update",
      "id": "CMD-RENDER",
      "expected_version": 2,
      "expected_hash": "sha256:old999...",
      "fields": {
        "body": "Updated render command description...",
        "hash": "sha256:new888..."
      }
    },
    {
      "op": "delete",
      "id": "CMD-OLD",
      "expected_version": 1,
      "mode": "deprecate"
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: state.snapshot.json — JSON Schema
docs.text: The snapshot is a point-in-time export of the full state. It is updated by apply and used by the planner to compute diffs.

docs.title: Top-Level Schema
docs.text:
{
  "$schema": "https://speqlite.dev/schemas/snapshot/v1.json",
  "version": 1,
  "snapshot_hash": "<sha256 of content>",
  "taken_at": "<ISO-8601 UTC>",
  "specs": [ ... ],
  "relations": [ ... ],
  "constraints": [ ... ]
}

docs.title: Spec Entry in Snapshot
docs.text:
{
  "id": "CMD-IMPORT",
  "title": "Import command",
  "kind": "command",
  "status": "fixed",
  "version": 3,
  "hash": "sha256:abc123...",
  "created_at": "2024-01-10T09:00:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
}

Note: body is not included in the snapshot (it is in SQLite). The snapshot carries only the metadata needed for diff computation. This keeps the snapshot file small and Git-diffable.

docs.title: Relation Entry in Snapshot
docs.text: {"from_id": "CMD-APPLY", "relation": "depends_on", "to_id": "CMD-PLAN"}

docs.title: Constraint Entry in Snapshot
docs.text: {"id": "CONSTR-001", "target_id": "FR-001", "language": "natural", "expression": "..."}

docs.title: Conflict Detection
docs.text:
When apply is called, it compares the current snapshot_hash in state.sqlite against the snapshot_hash recorded in state.plan.json.

If they differ, a concurrent mutation has occurred since the plan was generated. The apply engine aborts with exit code 4.

Additionally, for each update and transition operation, the apply engine verifies expected_version matches the current version in state.sqlite. If any version has changed, the operation is a conflict.

To resolve conflicts: 1. Inspect the current state: speqlite state show <id> 2. Re-run speqlite import to regenerate the plan against current state 3. Review the new plan: speqlite plan 4. Apply: speqlite apply

docs.title: Apply Engine Execution Order
docs.text:
Operations within a plan are applied in the following order to satisfy referential integrity:

  1. create operations (all new specs created first)
  2. add_relation operations (relations referencing newly created specs)
  3. update operations
  4. transition operations
  5. remove_relation operations
  6. delete (deprecate) operations

All operations execute within a single SQLite transaction. If any operation fails, the entire transaction is rolled back and the state is unchanged.

docs.title: Planner Diff Algorithm
docs.text:
The planner computes the minimal set of operations to transition from snapshot state to the normalizer's output.

func Plan(snapshot []SpecNode, desired []SpecNode) []Op:
    ops = []

    snapshotByID = index(snapshot, by=id)
    desiredByID  = index(desired,  by=id)

    for node in desired:
        existing = snapshotByID[node.id]
        if existing == nil:
            ops += CreateOp(node)
        elif existing.hash != node.hash:
            ops += UpdateOp(existing, node)
        // else: unchanged, no op

    for node in snapshot:
        if desiredByID[node.id] == nil:
            if node.status != "deprecated":
                ops += DeleteOp(node)   // mode=deprecate

    // Relation diff
    snapshotRels = set(snapshot.relations)
    desiredRels  = set(desired.relations)
    for rel in desiredRels - snapshotRels:
        ops += AddRelationOp(rel)
    for rel in snapshotRels - desiredRels:
        ops += RemoveRelationOp(rel)

    return ops

The planner never generates transition operations automatically — those are only produced by explicit speqlite state transition calls.

docs.title: State Machine
docs.text: Every specification node has a status field that represents its position in a defined lifecycle. Transitions between states are explicit, validated, and recorded in the event log.

docs.title: States
docs.text: State Description draft Under active authoring. Content is unstable and subject to change. review Submitted for review. Edits require an explicit transition back to draft first. fixed Agreed upon by stakeholders. Considered stable. implemented The corresponding artefact (code, configuration, document) exists. verified Independently confirmed correct by test, audit, or formal check. deprecated Superseded or no longer applicable. Retained for history.

docs.title: Transition Diagram
docs.text:
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> draft : create_spec

    draft --> review : submit_for_review
    draft --> deprecated : deprecate

    review --> draft : request_changes
    review --> fixed : approve
    review --> deprecated : deprecate

    fixed --> implemented : mark_implemented
    fixed --> review : reopen
    fixed --> deprecated : deprecate

    implemented --> verified : verify
    implemented --> fixed : revert_to_fixed
    implemented --> deprecated : deprecate

    verified --> deprecated : deprecate
    verified --> implemented : unverify

    deprecated --> draft : reactivate

docs.title: Valid Transitions Table
docs.text:
From To Trigger Notes draft review submit_for_review Locks the spec from further edits until reviewed draft deprecated deprecate Abandoning a draft review draft request_changes Reviewer sends it back for rework review fixed approve Stakeholder approval review deprecated deprecate Rejected during review fixed implemented mark_implemented Code/artefact created fixed review reopen Re-evaluation needed fixed deprecated deprecate Superseded before implementation implemented verified verify Acceptance test or audit passed implemented fixed revert_to_fixed Implementation reverted implemented deprecated deprecate Implementation abandoned verified deprecated deprecate Spec removed from active scope verified implemented unverify Verification retracted deprecated draft reactivate Resurrecting a deprecated spec

Any transition not listed above is invalid and will be rejected by the validator and apply engine.

docs.title: Rule 1: No arbitrary skipping
docs.text: You cannot jump from draft directly to verified. Every transition must follow an allowed edge in the graph. The apply engine enforces this.

docs.title: Rule 2: Deprecation is always available
docs.text: Any spec in any non-deprecated state can be deprecated. Deprecated specs are not deleted; they remain in state for history and relation resolution.

docs.title: Rule 3: Reactivation always returns to draft
docs.text: When a deprecated spec is reactivated, it returns to draft, not to whatever state it was in before deprecation. This prevents accidental promotion of stale specs.

docs.title: Rule 4: fixed is the canonical stable state
docs.text: For dependency tracking and formal export purposes, fixed is the minimum status for a spec to be considered stable. The validator warns when a depends_on relation targets a spec still in draft or review.

docs.title: Rule 5: verified has special validation semantics
docs.text: A spec in verified status may carry formal constraint expressions in the constraints table. The export pipeline will only emit constraints for verified specs (unless --include-all is passed).

docs.title: Event Log Entries for Transitions
docs.text:
Every status transition writes a change_status event to the event log:

{
  "event_type": "change_status",
  "spec_id": "CMD-IMPORT",
  "payload_json": {
    "from_status": "draft",
    "to_status": "review",
    "transition": "submit_for_review",
    "actor": "cli"
  },
  "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
}

docs.title: Validation Errors Related to State
docs.text:
Error Code Description E_INVALID_TRANSITION Attempted transition is not in the valid edges table E_DRAFT_DEPENDENCY A fixed/implemented/verified spec depends on a draft spec E_DEPRECATED_DEPENDENCY A non-deprecated spec depends on a deprecated spec E_SELF_TRANSITION from_status == to_status (no-op; rejected)

See CLI Reference for the validate command and Error Catalogue for full error definitions.

docs.title: CLI Commands for Status Management
docs.text:
# Transition a spec to review
speqlite state transition CMD-IMPORT --to review

# Approve a spec (review → fixed)
speqlite state transition CMD-IMPORT --to fixed

# Mark as implemented
speqlite state transition CMD-IMPORT --to implemented

# Deprecate
speqlite state transition CMD-IMPORT --to deprecated

# Bulk status listing
speqlite state list --status draft
speqlite state list --status review

All transitions go through the plan/apply workflow: state transition generates a plan entry; apply commits it.
